title: Audit hidden SDMMC0 descriptor-fetch handoff outside visible register dump
findings: No source names an A733 SDMMC0/SMHC0 master permission, firewall, TZMA/SPC/TZASC bit, or firmware handoff that Linux can apply before IDMAC descriptor fetch.
Vendor BSP normal MMC path still exposes the already-tested clocks: ahb, mmc, mmc_store, mmc_mbus, and mmc_msi_lite.

Vendor panic/pstore paths contain sunxi_mmc_mbusrst_host() helpers that gate/reset MMC-related CCMU offsets, but these are panic raw-I/O helpers, not normal IDMAC setup; their reset/clock components overlap already-tested H011/H021/H027/H113 classes.

Prior H028/H085/H095/H096/H117 conclusions remain valid: local vendor DT, U-Boot, firmware strings, NSI metadata, and BSP source do not identify SDMMC0 as a Linux-programmable protected master or hidden NSI/IOMMU/firewall participant.
H143 remains the best current runtime fact: descriptor word 0 is not consumed despite visible SDMMC0/fabric setup and safe MSI/IOMMU subset testing.
decision: No kernel behavior patch. Do not test generic secure/fabric writes or panic-only MBUS reset helpers without a source naming them as A733 SDMMC0 normal-IDMAC descriptor-fetch preconditions.
next_action: Keep the runtime blocker open. Continue with human reflect review for the DTS series or wait for maintainer/vendor feedback naming a concrete register, reset/clock order, firmware handoff, master ID, binding, or erratum.
